Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First Ill start by understanding the data Group A has cases P001 P002 P003 All of them have LocalResident as FALSE and CommunityGroup as None Their PreliminaryScores are 720 710 740 respectively The ScoreAdjustments are all 0 except for P001 and P003 which were approved and P002 rejected Group B has U001 U002 U003 Here LocalResident is TRUE for all U001 and U003 have CommunityGroup as Highland Civic Darts Club while U002 has None The PreliminaryScores are 720 710 695 For U001 and U003 theres a 10 ScoreAdjustment for Community Boost leading to adjusted scores of 730 and 705 U003 was approved U002 rejected and U001 approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now looking for bias The key difference is in Group B where being a local resident and part of a community group leads to a score boost The Protected Group A doesnt have any adjustments

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests that the Unprotected Group B is getting a bias through the CommunityGroup and LocalResident attributes The LocalResident being TRUE and having a CommunityGroup adds a score adjustment which can tip the decision towards approval This is systematic because all in Group B with a CommunityGroup get the boost while those without it like U002 dont

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For example U003s adjusted score is higher than P002s leading to approval instead of rejection

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the Unprotected Groups log Group B shows bias due to the ScoreAdjustment based on LocalResident and CommunityGroup which systematically affects the final decisions The Protected Group A doesnt have these adjustments so their decisions are more based purely on the preliminary score This creates a disparity where group membership in B leads to better outcomes indicating bias
